steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the speaker 'indicates that several beneficial opportunities have recently become available to women.' The correct quote must show specific fields or domains newly opening up to women — not ambition, rights claims, or unrelated topics.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '"For worlds to conquer [woman] had not yearned, / Till [man] spoke of her feminine sphere as 'narrow.'"': Describes ambition prompted by external criticism, not opportunities becoming available. ✗
  • Choice B — '"Trade and science and craft and art, / Have opened their doors to the call of woman…"': 'Opened their doors' directly illustrates opportunities becoming newly available, and names four specific fields — trade, science, craft, and art. This matches both the 'recently available' and 'beneficial' language of the claim. ✓
  • Choice C — '"[Woman] grasped the scope of the First Intent / That made her kingdom for her, no other."': Asserts an inherent domain belonging to women, not opportunities recently opened. ✗
  • Choice D — '"Time has cut his beard… Ah! But life is joy."': From a different poem entirely and addresses an unrelated subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
